assertions.expectation: Recommends Stable or Extended release channel to minimize upgrade frequency
assertions.reason: The model explicitly recommends the Stable release channel to ensure thoroughly vetted versions and minimize churn.

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' maintenance exclusion to block disruptive upgrades while allowing security patches on the control plane
assertions.reason: The model recommends using the 'no_minor_or_node_upgrades' exclusion scope, explaining that it freezes node pools while still allowing control plane security patches.

assertions.expectation: Recommends a narrow maintenance window (e.g., weekends only) so CP patches happen at predictable times
assertions.reason: The model provides a gcloud command setting an 8-hour off-peak window specifically restricted to weekends (FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=SA,SU).

assertions.expectation: Suggests isolating training workloads on dedicated GPU node pools separate from serving/infrastructure pools
assertions.reason: While the model names a pool 'h100-training-pool', it does not explicitly suggest the architectural best practice of separating or isolating these from serving or infrastructure node pools.

assertions.expectation: Recommends disabling auto-upgrade on GPU training node pools and upgrading manually during scheduled gaps between training runs
assertions.reason: The model explains how to use maintenance exclusions to effectively halt auto-upgrades, and outlines a specific process to 'Manually trigger the minor/node upgrade' between multi-week runs.

assertions.expectation: Advises configuring training frameworks with checkpointing so jobs can resume after any disruption
assertions.reason: The model includes a 'Mandatory Checkpointing' section, advising that the LLM training framework should frequently save checkpoints to Cloud Storage to handle unexpected hardware failures.

assertions.expectation: Mentions GPU driver version testing — always validate in staging before applying to production GPU pools
assertions.reason: The model explicitly states to 'Always validate the target GKE version on a small staging node pool before lifting your production maintenance exclusion' due to GPU driver changes.

assertions.expectation: Notes that GPUDirect/RDMA networking has strict GKE version requirements — verify compatibility before upgrading
assertions.reason: The model notes that high-performance networking like GPUDirect-TCPX is tightly coupled to GKE/CUDA versions and advises verifying RDMA/GPUDirect connectivity upon upgrading.
